gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/285)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/285)
text: قُلْتُ لَهُ رَجُلٌ لِي عَلَيْهِ دَرَاهِمُ وَ كَانَتْ دَارُهُ رَهْناً فَأَرَدْتُ أَنْ أَبِيعَهَا قَالَ أُعِيذُكَ بِاللَّهِ أَنْ تُخْرِجَهُ مِنْ ظِلِّ رَأْسِهِ .

en.hubeali: Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Fazzal, from Ibrahim Bin Usman,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, said, ‘I said to him<sup>asws</sup>, ‘There were some Dirhams for me upon a man, and his house was a mortgage, so I intended to sell it’. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘I<sup>asws</sup> seek Refuge for you with Allah<sup>azwj</sup> that you would remove the shade from his head’.
en.sarwar: 21. Ahmad ibn Muhammad has narrated from ibn Faddal from Ibrahim ibn ‘Uthman who has said the following: “I once said to abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, ‘There is a man who owes to me a certain amount of dirhams and his house is as al-Rahn (security deposit) with me and I want to sell it. He (the Imam) said, ‘Do you, by Allah, have the intention to take the roof away from the top of his head?”’
